{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Tsuyama, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miwayama Kofun cluster | native_name =美和山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miwayama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miwayama Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miwayama Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217359|title=美和山古墳群|trans-title= Miwayama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped burial mounds in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[Innoshō Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]].
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Miwayama Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size |- |Miwayama No.1 {{nihongo|'''Dozuka'''|胴塚||}}||{{coords|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||80.5 m long, x 48 m circular portion, 5.3 to 6 m high |- |Miwayama No.2 {{nihongo|'''Jazuka'''|蛇塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|43.01|N|133|57|58.02|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 34m dia x 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.3 {{nihongo|'''Mimizuka'''|耳塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|41.03|N|133|57|57.44|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 36m dia x 5.5 to 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.6 ||{{coords|35|3|44.07|N|133|57|59.03|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 17m dia x 2.0 m high |- |}
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Miwayama Kofun Group-1, kouenbu.jpg|Miwayama No.1 anterior towards posterior File:Miwayama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|Miwayama No.2 File:Miwayama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Miwayama No.3 File:Miwayama Kofun Group-6, funkyu.jpg|Miwayama No.6 </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Tsuyama, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miwayama Kofun cluster | native_name =美和山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miwayama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miwayama Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miwayama Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217359|title=美和山古墳群|trans-title= Miwayama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped burial mounds in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[Innoshō Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]].

The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217359|title=美和山古墳群|trans-title= Miwayama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped burial mounds in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[Innoshō Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period burial mound cluster in Tsuyama, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miwayama Kofun cluster | native_name =美和山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miwayama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miwayama Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miwayama Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217359|title=美和山古墳群|trans-title= Miwayama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped burial mounds in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[Innoshō Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]].
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Miwayama Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size |- |Miwayama No.1 {{nihongo|'''Dozuka'''|胴塚||}}||{{coords|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||80.5 m long, x 48 m circular portion, 5.3 to 6 m high |- |Miwayama No.2 {{nihongo|'''Jazuka'''|蛇塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|43.01|N|133|57|58.02|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 34m dia x 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.3 {{nihongo|'''Mimizuka'''|耳塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|41.03|N|133|57|57.44|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 36m dia x 5.5 to 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.6 ||{{coords|35|3|44.07|N|133|57|59.03|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 17m dia x 2.0 m high |- |}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Tsuyama, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miwayama Kofun cluster | native_name =美和山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miwayama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miwayama Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miwayama Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217359|title=美和山古墳群|trans-title= Miwayama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[Innoshō Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]].
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Miwayama Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size |- |Miwayama No.1 {{nihongo|'''Dozuka'''|胴塚||}}||{{coords|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||80.5 m long, x 48 m circular portion, 5.3 to 6 m high |- |Miwayama No.2 {{nihongo|'''Jazuka'''|蛇塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|43.01|N|133|57|58.02|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 34m dia x 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.3 {{nihongo|'''Mimizuka'''|耳塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|41.03|N|133|57|57.44|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 36m dia x 5.5 to 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.6 ||{{coords|35|3|44.07|N|133|57|59.03|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 17m dia x 2.0 m high |- |}

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Tsuyama, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miwayama Kofun cluster | native_name =美和山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miwayama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miwayama Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miwayama Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217359|title=美和山古墳群|trans-title= Miwayama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[Innoshō Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Tsuyama, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miwayama Kofun cluster | native_name =美和山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miwayama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miwayama Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miwayama Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217359|title=美和山古墳群|trans-title= Miwayama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[Innoshō Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]].
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Miwayama Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size |- |Miwayama No.1 {{nihongo|'''Dozuka'''|胴塚||}}||{{coords|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||80.5 m long, x 48 m circular portion, 5.3 to 6 m high |- |Miwayama No.2 {{nihongo|'''Jazuka'''|蛇塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|43.01|N|133|57|58.02|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 34m dia x 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.3 {{nihongo|'''Mimizuka'''|耳塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|41.03|N|133|57|57.44|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 36m dia x 5.5 to 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.6 ||{{coords|35|3|44.07|N|133|57|59.03|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 17m dia x 2.0 m high |- |}
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Miwayama Kofun Group-1, kouenbu.jpg|Miwayama No.1 anterior towards posterior File:Miwayama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|Miwayama No.2 File:Miwayama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Miwayama No.3 File:Miwayama Kofun Group-6, funkyu.jpg|Miwayama No.6 </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Tsuyama, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miwayama Kofun cluster | native_name =美和山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miwayama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miwayama Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miwayama Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217359|title=美和山古墳群|trans-title= Miwayama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[Innoshō Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]].

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Tsuyama, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miwayama Kofun cluster | native_name =美和山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miwayama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miwayama Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miwayama Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217359|title=美和山古墳群|trans-title= Miwayama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[Innoshō Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]].
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Miwayama Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size |- |Miwayama No.1 {{nihongo|'''Dozuka'''|胴塚||}}||{{coords|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||80.5 m long, x 48 m circular portion, 5.3 to 6 m high |- |Miwayama No.2 {{nihongo|'''Jazuka'''|蛇塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|43.01|N|133|57|58.02|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 34m dia x 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.3 {{nihongo|'''Mimizuka'''|耳塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|41.03|N|133|57|57.44|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 36m dia x 5.5 to 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.6 ||{{coords|35|3|44.07|N|133|57|59.03|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 17m dia x 2.0 m high |- |}

It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[Innoshō Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Tsuyama, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miwayama Kofun cluster | native_name =美和山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miwayama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miwayama Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miwayama Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217359|title=美和山古墳群|trans-title= Miwayama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[Innoshō Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kishin Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Miwayama Kofun Group]][[en:Miwayama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:美和山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28685450]] Wikidata name: Miwayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Tsuyama, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miwayama Kofun cluster | native_name =美和山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miwayama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miwayama Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miwayama Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.06306667|N|133.966475|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217359|title=美和山古墳群|trans-title= Miwayama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Innoshō Station|Innoshō Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kishin Line|Kishin Line]].
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Miwayama Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size |- |Miwayama No.1 {{nihongo|'''Dozuka'''|胴塚||}}||{{coords|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||80.5 m long, x 48 m circular portion, 5.3 to 6 m high |- |Miwayama No.2 {{nihongo|'''Jazuka'''|蛇塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|43.01|N|133|57|58.02|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 34m dia x 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.3 {{nihongo|'''Mimizuka'''|耳塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|41.03|N|133|57|57.44|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 36m dia x 5.5 to 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.6 ||{{coords|35|3|44.07|N|133|57|59.03|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 17m dia x 2.0 m high |- |}
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Miwayama Kofun Group-1, kouenbu.jpg|Miwayama No.1 anterior towards posterior File:Miwayama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|Miwayama No.2 File:Miwayama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Miwayama No.3 File:Miwayama Kofun Group-6, funkyu.jpg|Miwayama No.6 </gallery>

The three others are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Innoshō Station|Innoshō Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kishin Line|Kishin Line]].

The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. ==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Innoshō Station|Innoshō Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kishin Line|Kishin Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miwayama Kofun Group]][[en:Miwayama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:美和山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28685450]] Wikidata name: Miwayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Tsuyama, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miwayama Kofun cluster | native_name =美和山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miwayama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miwayama Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miwayama Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.06306667|N|133.966475|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217359|title=美和山古墳群|trans-title= Miwayama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Innoshō Station|Innoshō Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kishin Line|Kishin Line]].
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Miwayama Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size |- |Miwayama No.1 {{nihongo|'''Dozuka'''|胴塚||}}||{{coords|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||80.5 m long, x 48 m circular portion, 5.3 to 6 m high |- |Miwayama No.2 {{nihongo|'''Jazuka'''|蛇塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|43.01|N|133|57|58.02|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 34m dia x 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.3 {{nihongo|'''Mimizuka'''|耳塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|41.03|N|133|57|57.44|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 36m dia x 5.5 to 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.6 ||{{coords|35|3|44.07|N|133|57|59.03|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 17m dia x 2.0 m high |- |}
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Miwayama Kofun Group-1, kouenbu.jpg|Miwayama No.1 anterior towards posterior File:Miwayama Kofun Group-2, funkyu.jpg|Miwayama No.2 File:Miwayama Kofun Group-3, funkyu.jpg|Miwayama No.3 File:Miwayama Kofun Group-6, funkyu.jpg|Miwayama No.6 </gallery>

==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Innoshō Station|Innoshō Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kishin Line|Kishin Line]].

The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. ==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Innoshō Station|Innoshō Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kishin Line|Kishin Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miwayama Kofun Group]][[en:Miwayama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:美和山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28685450]] Wikidata name: Miwayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Tsuyama, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miwayama Kofun cluster | native_name =美和山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miwayama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miwayama Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miwayama Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.06306667|N|133.966475|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217359|title=美和山古墳群|trans-title= Miwayama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Innoshō Station|Innoshō Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kishin Line|Kishin Line]].
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Miwayama Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size |- |Miwayama No.1 {{nihongo|'''Dozuka'''|胴塚||}}||{{coords|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||80.5 m long, x 48 m circular portion, 5.3 to 6 m high |- |Miwayama No.2 {{nihongo|'''Jazuka'''|蛇塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|43.01|N|133|57|58.02|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 34m dia x 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.3 {{nihongo|'''Mimizuka'''|耳塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|41.03|N|133|57|57.44|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 36m dia x 5.5 to 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.6 ||{{coords|35|3|44.07|N|133|57|59.03|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 17m dia x 2.0 m high |- |}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Miwayama Kofun Group]][[en:Miwayama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:美和山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q28685450]] Wikidata name: Miwayama Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun cluster in Tsuyama, Okayama, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Miwayama Kofun cluster | native_name =美和山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Miwayama Kofun Group-1, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Miwayama Kofun No.1 | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Miwayama Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|display=inline}}; {{Coord|35.06306667|N|133.966475|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217359|title=美和山古墳群|trans-title= Miwayama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Innoshō Station|Innoshō Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kishin Line|Kishin Line]].
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Miwayama Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size |- |Miwayama No.1 {{nihongo|'''Dozuka'''|胴塚||}}||{{coords|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||80.5 m long, x 48 m circular portion, 5.3 to 6 m high |- |Miwayama No.2 {{nihongo|'''Jazuka'''|蛇塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|43.01|N|133|57|58.02|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 34m dia x 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.3 {{nihongo|'''Mimizuka'''|耳塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|41.03|N|133|57|57.44|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 36m dia x 5.5 to 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.6 ||{{coords|35|3|44.07|N|133|57|59.03|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 17m dia x 2.0 m high |- |}

One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Innoshō Station|Innoshō Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kishin Line|Kishin Line]].

==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Innoshō Station|Innoshō Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kishin Line|Kishin Line]].

{{Coord|35.06306667|N|133.966475|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama, Okayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217359|title=美和山古墳群|trans-title= Miwayama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Innoshō Station|Innoshō Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kishin Line|Kishin Line]].

One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Miwayama Kofun cluster'''|美和山古墳群| Miwayama Kofun-gun|}} is a group of ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in Ninomiya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Tsuyama, Okayama|Tsuyama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a National Historic Site in 2015.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217359|title=美和山古墳群|trans-title= Miwayama Kofun-gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2022}}</ref>
==Background== The Miyayama Kofun cluster is located on a hill along the left bank of the Yoshii River to the west of the Tsuyama urban area. The site was initially scheduled to be razed for a housing estate in 1977, but after the discovery of the ''kofun'' cluster, a 30,000 square meter area was quickly placed under protection as a National Historic Site for preservation. Within this area are four surviving tumuli. One is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a length of 80 meters, making it one of the largest keyhole-shaped Kofuns in the [[:en:Mimasaka Province|Mimasaka region]]. The three others are all {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). ''[[Fukiishi]]'' remain on some areas of the tumuli. Artifacts [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] include ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' fragments with patterns drawn by a spatula. From these artifacts, the tumuli are believed to have been built in the 4th century. The tumuli themselves have not been excavated, so details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s are unknown.
Miwayama Castle was built on this site during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] (14th to 16th centuries), and the east and west overhangs of Miwayama No. 1 are believed to be the remains of Miwayama Castle's earthen walls.
The site is maintained as a park, and is located 15 minutes on foot from [[:en:Innoshō Station|Innoshō Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kishin Line|Kishin Line]].
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" |+ Miwayama Kofun cluster !name !location !type !Size |- |Miwayama No.1 {{nihongo|'''Dozuka'''|胴塚||}}||{{coords|35|3|47.04|N|133|57|59.31|E|region:JP-33_type:landmark|}}||keyhole||80.5 m long, x 48 m circular portion, 5.3 to 6 m high |- |Miwayama No.2 {{nihongo|'''Jazuka'''|蛇塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|43.01|N|133|57|58.02|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 34m dia x 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.3 {{nihongo|'''Mimizuka'''|耳塚||}} ||{{coords|35|3|41.03|N|133|57|57.44|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 36m dia x 5.5 to 6.5m high |- |Miwayama No.6 ||{{coords|35|3|44.07|N|133|57|59.03|E|type:landmark|}}||circular|| 17m dia x 2.0 m high |- |}
